framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Regulations regarding the scheduling and management of flight crew duty times to ensure safety and mitigate fatigue.
policies.scope: All operators and flight crew members
policies.objective: To establish guidelines for flight duty periods, rest requirements, and fatigue management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Standard allowance added at the end of flight time for completion of checks and post flight duties.
subpolicies.control: Defined in the FDTL Scheme of the operator.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators shall define eight hours of local night for seasonal schedules.
subpolicies.control: May cater for type of aircraft/Fleet type.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators must not schedule crew members known or suspected to be fatigued.
subpolicies.control: Executive responsibility defined by the operator.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators must publish the Flight Crew Roster sufficiently in advance including weekly rest.
subpolicies.control: To ensure compliance with duty time regulations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators must define a policy for assigning temporary home bases to flight crew.
subpolicies.control: Minimum duration of 7 days and maximum of 28 days.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators should balance commercial imperatives with crew members’ ability to work effectively.
subpolicies.control: Follow principles for adequate rest and workload distribution.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establish a clear policy for raising and responding to fatigue reports.
subpolicies.control: Non-punitive and confidentiality policy for fatigue reports.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Flight Time and Duty Period Limits: Two Pilot Operation
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Maximum flight time and duty period limits for two pilot operations.
subpolicies.control: Defined in specified tables.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Flight Time and Duty Period Limits: Augmented Crew
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Maximum flight time and duty period limits for augmented crew operations.
subpolicies.control: Defined in specified tables.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Maximum Cumulative Flight Time and Duty Period Limitations
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Cumulative limits on flight time and duty periods over various time frames.
subpolicies.control: Defined in specified tables.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for split duty breaks and their impact on flight duty periods.
subpolicies.control: Conditions under which breaks are taken and accommodation provided.

policies.policy_description: Regulations governing the flight duty period and associated rest periods for flight crew.
policies.objective: To ensure adequate rest and compliance with duty time regulations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the minimum rest periods required before and after flight duty.
subpolicies.control: Includes specific rest durations based on duty periods and time zone crossings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulations regarding standby periods and their impact on duty time.
subpolicies.control: Details the maximum duration of standby and how it counts towards duty.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for counting positioning time as duty.
subpolicies.control: Specifies conditions under which positioning time is counted towards flight duty.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Restrictions on flight crew duties during consecutive night operations.
subpolicies.control: Limits flight duties between 0000 to 0600 hours local time.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulations regarding training flights conducted by flight crew.
subpolicies.control: Specifies how training flights count towards duty time and additional rest requirements.

policies.policy_description: Regulations and requirements for operating Ultra Long Range flights.
policies.scope: Applicable to operators conducting ULR flights.
policies.objective: To ensure crew alertness and proper rest during ULR operations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum rest periods for crew members before and after ULR flights.
subpolicies.control: Crew must be provided with minimum rest periods as specified.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for the number and qualifications of pilots on ULR flights.
subpolicies.control: Each ULR flight must have at least four pilots, with specific qualifications.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidance for flight crew on pre-flight preparations and in-flight rest.
subpolicies.control: In-flight rest plan must include at least one rest period of four hours.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Rest period requirements for flight crew returning from ULR flights.
subpolicies.control: Crew must receive at least 120 hours of rest after ULR flights.

policies.policy_description: Regulations for extending flight time and duty periods due to unforeseen circumstances.
policies.objective: To manage flight time and duty period extensions safely.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Flight Time and Duty Period Extensions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which flight time and duty periods may be extended.
subpolicies.control: Extensions are subject to specific limits and conditions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjustments to rest periods based on extensions of flight time or duty periods.
subpolicies.control: Rest periods must be increased based on the extent of extensions.

policies.policy_title: Maintenance of Records and Monitoring System
policies.policy_description: Requirements for maintaining records of flight crew duties and rest periods.
policies.scope: Applicable to operators and flight crew members.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate tracking of flight and duty records for compliance and safety.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Duration for which records must be maintained.
subpolicies.control: Records must be kept for 18 months.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specific information to be included in crew records.
subpolicies.control: Records must include flight duty periods, rest periods, and extensions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for reviewing crew scheduling based on extensions.
subpolicies.control: Operators must review schedules if extensions exceed specified thresholds.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for flight crew to maintain personal records of their duties.
subpolicies.control: Flight crew must keep personal records of flight time and rest periods.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Establishes minimum rest requirements for flight crew before and after duty periods.
policies.objective: To ensure adequate rest for flight crew to maintain safety and performance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines minimum rest periods before undertaking a flight duty period.
subpolicies.control: Minimum rest must be at least as long as the preceding duty period or 12, 18, or 36 hours based on time zone crossings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies rest requirements after standby periods.
subpolicies.control: 12 hours rest after standby; if standby culminates in duty, rest is based on total duty time.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mandates a minimum weekly rest period for flight crew.
subpolicies.control: Minimum of 48 continuous hours including two local nights, with specific conditions for increased rest.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Rest After Return to Home Base
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensures additional rest for flight crew returning to home base after crossing time zones.
subpolicies.control: Minimum rest based on time zone differences.

policies.policy_description: Outlines the rules and conditions for standby periods for flight crew.
policies.objective: To regulate standby periods and their impact on duty time.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishes guidelines for including standby in rosters.
subpolicies.control: Standby must be notified and not exceed specified hours.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Sets maximum limits for standby periods at the airport and at home.
subpolicies.control: Standby at airport not to exceed 8 hours; specific rules for duty culmination.

policies.policy_description: Defines the rules regarding positioning time for flight crew.
policies.objective: To ensure proper accounting of positioning time as duty.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies that all time spent on positioning counts as duty.
subpolicies.control: Positioning time must be counted towards flight duty periods.

policies.policy_description: Regulates flight duty for crew during consecutive night operations.
policies.objective: To limit fatigue associated with consecutive night flights.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Limits flight duty between 0000 to 0600 hours to two consecutive nights.
subpolicies.control: Specific conditions for scheduling flights after consecutive night duties.

policies.policy_description: Outlines the regulations for training flights conducted by flight crew.
policies.objective: To ensure training flights are properly accounted for in duty time.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies that all training flight time counts towards duty time.
subpolicies.control: Includes specific provisions for base training flights.

policies.policy_description: Regulations and requirements for operating Ultra Long Range (ULR) flights.
policies.scope: Applicable to operators conducting ULR flights.
policies.objective: To ensure crew alertness and safety during ULR flights.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators must adopt a Flight Risk Management System (FRMS) for monitoring crew alertness.
subpolicies.control: DGCA validation of flights using prescriptive regulations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Provision of designated rest facilities on board aircraft.
subpolicies.control: Facilities must include at least two independent rest areas with horizontal bunks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for the number and qualifications of flight crew on ULR flights.
subpolicies.control: Each ULR flight must have at least four pilots, including two qualified Pilot-in-Command.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for pre-flight preparations and in-flight rest for flight crew.
subpolicies.control: In-flight rest plan must include at least one rest period of no less than four hours.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Rest requirements for flight crew after ULR flights.
subpolicies.control: Crew must have at least 120 hours free of all duties after returning to home base.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which flight time and duty periods may be extended.
subpolicies.control: Extensions allowed under specific limits and conditions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for maintaining records of flight crew duties and rest periods.
subpolicies.control: Records must be kept for 18 months and include specific details for each crew member.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implementation of a computerized system for record maintenance.
subpolicies.control: System must ensure access is restricted to designated officers and include an audit trail.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Regulations regarding the scheduling and management of flight crew duty times to ensure safety and mitigate fatigue.
policies.scope: Applicable to all operators and flight crew members.
policies.objective: To ensure that flight crew members are not fatigued and that their duty periods are within safe limits.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Standard allowance added at the end of flight time for completion of checks and records.
subpolicies.control: Specified in the FDTL Scheme of the operator.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators shall define an eight-hour local night to cater for seasonal schedules.
subpolicies.control: May cater for type of aircraft/Fleet type.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators shall not require a flight crew member to perform duties if they are known or suspected to be fatigued.
subpolicies.control: Safety of flight must not be adversely affected.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Each Operator shall publish the Flight Crew Roster sufficiently in advance including weekly rest.
subpolicies.control: Ensures crew members are informed of their schedules.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators shall define a policy for assigning temporary home bases to flight crew.
subpolicies.control: Minimum duration of 7 days and maximum of 28 days.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators should balance commercial imperatives with crew members’ ability to work effectively.
subpolicies.control: Includes principles for adequate rest and distribution of duties.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establish a clear policy for fatigue reports, including how to raise and respond to them.
subpolicies.control: Executive responsibility for addressing fatigue management defined by the operator.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Flight Time and Duty Period Limits
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Maximum flight time and duty periods for two-pilot operations and augmented crew.
subpolicies.control: Limits specified in tables within the policy.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulations regarding breaks during split duty and their impact on flight duty period extensions.
subpolicies.control: Conditions for breaks and accommodations provided.

policies.policy_description: Regulations governing the Flight Duty Period (FDP) and associated rest periods for flight crew.
policies.scope: Applicable to all flight crew members and operators.
policies.objective: To ensure safe flight operations by regulating duty and rest periods.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the minimum rest periods required before and after flight duty.
Regulations regarding standby periods and their impact on duty time.
Guidelines for counting positioning time as duty and its implications.
Restrictions on flight duty during consecutive nights.
Policies governing the counting of training flights towards duty time.

policies.policy_description: Regulations and requirements for operating Ultra Long Range flights.
policies.scope: Applicable to operators conducting ULR flights.
policies.objective: To ensure crew alertness and safety during ULR flights.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum rest periods for crew members before and after ULR flights.
subpolicies.control: Crew members must have a minimum rest period as specified.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for the number and qualifications of pilots on ULR flights.
subpolicies.control: Each ULR flight must have at least four pilots, with specific qualifications.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for pre-flight preparations and in-flight rest for flight crew.
subpolicies.control: Flight crew must be appropriately rested, with at least one rest period of four hours.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Rest period requirements for flight crew after completing ULR flights.
subpolicies.control: Crew must have at least 120 hours free of all duties after returning to home base.

policies.policy_description: Guidelines for extending flight time and duty periods due to unforeseen circumstances.
policies.objective: To manage crew workload and ensure safety during unforeseen operational events.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Flight Time and Duty Period Extensions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which flight time and duty periods may be extended.
subpolicies.control: Extensions are limited to specified maximums and require additional rest.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for crew consent and reporting for extended flights.
subpolicies.control: PIC must consult with crew and inform the Head of Operations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Reporting requirements for extensions granted under unforeseen circumstances.
subpolicies.control: Head of Operations must submit quarterly reports to DGCA.

policies.policy_title: Maintenance of Records and Monitoring System
policies.policy_description: Requirements for maintaining records of flight crew duties and rest periods.
policies.scope: Applicable to operators and flight crew members.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance and facilitate inspections by regulatory authorities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Duration and details of records to be maintained by operators.
subpolicies.control: Records must be kept for 18 months and include specific details for each crew member.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Tracking and reviewing instances of extended flight time and duty.
subpolicies.control: Operators must review schedules if extensions exceed specified thresholds.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for flight crew to maintain personal records of their duty and rest periods.
subpolicies.control: Crew members must keep accurate records through a computerized system.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Establishes minimum rest periods for flight crew before and after duty.
policies.objective: To ensure adequate rest for flight crew to maintain safety and performance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines minimum rest before flight duty based on duty period and time zone crossings.
Specifies rest requirements after standby periods.
Mandates a minimum of 48 hours weekly rest including local nights.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Rest After Return to Home Base
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Additional rest requirements for flight crew returning home after crossing time zones.

policies.policy_description: Regulates standby periods for flight crew.
policies.objective: To manage standby periods effectively within duty rosters.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Inclusion of standby in regular rosters and notification to crew.
Limits the duration of standby periods and conditions for duty counting.

policies.policy_description: Guidelines for positioning time as part of duty.
policies.objective: To ensure proper accounting of positioning time in duty calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All positioning time is counted as duty.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Positioning Time in Flight Duty Period
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Positioning time is included in flight duty period if directly preceding a flight.

policies.policy_description: Regulations for flight crew operating during consecutive night periods.
policies.objective: To limit consecutive night duties to ensure crew well-being.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Restrictions on flight duties between 0000 to 0600 hours.

policies.policy_description: Regulations regarding training flights conducted by flight crew.
policies.objective: To ensure that training flights are accounted for in duty time.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All time spent on training flights counts towards duty time.

policies.policy_description: Operators must submit a specific scheme for city pair approval in accordance with CAR provisions.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with regulatory requirements for ULR flights.

policies.policy_description: Operators are encouraged to adopt FRMS for monitoring crew alertness.
policies.objective: To enhance crew alertness during ULR flights.

policies.policy_description: Designated rest facilities must be provided on board aircraft for flight crew.
policies.objective: To ensure a conducive environment for crew rest.

policies.policy_title: Flight Crew Requirements for ULR Flights
policies.policy_description: Each ULR flight must be operated by at least four pilots, with specific qualifications.
policies.objective: To ensure adequate crew for ULR flights.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidance on pre-flight preparations and in-flight rest for flight crew.
subpolicies.control: Flight crew must be appropriately rested for ULR flights.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: At least one rest period of no less than four hours must be provided during the flight.
subpolicies.control: Ensure compliance with rest period requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Flight crew must have a minimum of 120 hours free of all duties after ULR flights.
subpolicies.control: Rest period increases by 24 hours after two consecutive ULR flights.

policies.policy_description: Flight time and duty periods may be extended under specific unforeseen circumstances.
policies.objective: To manage unforeseen operational challenges effectively.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Flight Time and Duty Period Extensions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specific limits on extensions for flight time and duty periods.
subpolicies.control: Cumulative limits apply to extensions over 28 consecutive days.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Increased rest periods required based on duty period and flight time extensions.
subpolicies.control: Rest increases by 2 or 4 hours based on extension duration.

policies.policy_title: Maintenance of Records and Monitoring System
policies.policy_description: Operators must maintain records of flight duties and rest periods for inspection.
policies.objective: To facilitate audits and ensure compliance with duty and rest requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Records must include details of flight duty periods, rest periods, and extensions.
subpolicies.control: Records to be kept for 18 months.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: A computerized system must ensure compliance with flight time and duty period regulations.
subpolicies.control: Access limited to designated officers with an audit trail.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Regulations governing the flight duty time and rest periods for flight crew members.
policies.scope: All operators and flight crew members
policies.objective: To ensure safety by managing crew fatigue and duty periods.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Standard allowance added at the end of flight time for completion of checks and records.
subpolicies.control: Specified in the FDTL Scheme of the operator.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators shall define eight hours of local night considering seasonal schedules.
subpolicies.control: May cater for type of aircraft/Fleet type.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The FDTL scheme must be submitted to DGCA for approval and included in the Operations Manual.
subpolicies.control: Approval from DGCA required.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators must not schedule flight crew members if they are known or suspected to be fatigued.
subpolicies.control: Safety of flight must not be adversely affected.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators must publish the Flight Crew Roster in advance, including weekly rest.
subpolicies.control: Sufficient advance notice required.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Each flight crew member must be assigned a Home Base.
subpolicies.control: Operators to define a Temporary Home Base Policy if applicable.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum and maximum duration for temporary home base assignments defined by operators.
subpolicies.control: Minimum 7 days, maximum 28 days.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators should balance commercial needs with crew effectiveness.
subpolicies.control: Follow principles for adequate rest and duty distribution.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establish a clear policy for raising and responding to fatigue reports.
subpolicies.control: Executive responsibility defined by the operator.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Fatigue reports and action taken must be preserved for a minimum of one year.
subpolicies.control: Reports to be available to DGCA upon request.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Fatigue reports must follow a non-punitive and confidentiality policy.
subpolicies.control: Confidentiality assured.

policies.policy_title: Flight Time and Duty Period Limits
policies.policy_description: Limits on flight time and duty periods for two-pilot operations.
policies.objective: To ensure safety and manage crew fatigue.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Maximum Flight Time and Duty Period
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines maximum flight time and duty periods during any 24 hours.
subpolicies.control: Specific limits outlined in the table.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Limits on cumulative flight time and duty periods over various time frames.
subpolicies.control: Defined for 7, 14, 28, 90, and 365 consecutive days.

policies.policy_description: Regulations governing split duty and associated breaks.
policies.scope: All operators and flight crew members
policies.objective: To manage crew fatigue during split duty.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines maximum extension of flight duty period based on break duration.
subpolicies.control: Specific rules for breaks less than 3 hours, between 3 and 10 hours, and over 10 hours.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operators must provide suitable accommodation for breaks exceeding 6 hours.
subpolicies.control: Accommodation required for breaks less than 6 hours.

policies.policy_description: Regulations regarding the Flight Duty Period (FDP) and associated rest periods for flight crew.
policies.objective: To ensure adequate rest and compliance with duty time regulations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the minimum rest periods required before and after flight duty.
subpolicies.control: Includes specific rest duration based on time zone crossings and preceding duty periods.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulations governing standby periods for flight crew.
subpolicies.control: Limits standby periods and specifies how they count towards duty time.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for counting positioning time as duty.
subpolicies.control: Positioning time must be included in duty calculations and is subject to specific conditions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Restrictions on flight duty during consecutive night operations.
subpolicies.control: Limits flight duty between 0000 to 0600 hours local time.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulations regarding training flights conducted by flight crew.
subpolicies.control: All training flights count towards flight duty time and have specific landing regulations.

policies.policy_description: Regulations and requirements for operating Ultra Long Range flights.
policies.scope: Applicable to operators conducting ULR flights.
policies.objective: To ensure crew alertness and proper rest for ULR flight operations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum rest periods for crew members before and after ULR flights.
subpolicies.control: Crew members must be provided with a minimum rest period as specified.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for the number and qualifications of pilots on ULR flights.
subpolicies.control: Each ULR flight must have at least four pilots, including two pilot-in-command qualified.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for pre-flight preparations and in-flight rest for flight crew.
subpolicies.control: Flight crew must be appropriately rested, with at least one rest period of four hours during the flight.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Rest period requirements for flight crew after completing ULR flights.
subpolicies.control: Flight crew must receive at least 120 hours of rest after returning to base, increasing with consecutive ULR flights.

policies.policy_description: Regulations for extending flight time and duty periods under unforeseen circumstances.
policies.objective: To provide guidelines for managing unforeseen operational circumstances affecting flight time and duty periods.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Flight Time and Duty Period Extensions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which flight time and duty periods may be extended.
subpolicies.control: Flight time may be extended by a maximum of 1 hour; FDP by a maximum of 2 hours.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjustments to rest periods based on extensions of flight time and duty periods.
subpolicies.control: Rest periods must be increased by 2 hours or 4 hours based on the extent of the extensions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for utilizing crew after reporting and during extended duty periods.
subpolicies.control: Crew can be utilized after a minimum of twice the time spent after reporting, subject to a minimum of 12 hours of rest.

policies.policy_title: Maintenance of Records and Monitoring System
policies.policy_description: Requirements for maintaining records of flight crew duties and rest periods.
policies.scope: Applicable to operators and flight crew members.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate record-keeping for flight crew duties and rest periods.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Duration for which records of duties and rest periods must be maintained.
subpolicies.control: Records must be kept for 18 months.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specific information that must be included in the records for each flight crew member.
subpolicies.control: Records must include start, duration, and end of flight duty periods, rest periods, and cumulative flight time.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for reviewing crew scheduling arrangements based on usage of discretion.
subpolicies.control: Operators must review and change schedules if discretion is used on more than 20% of occasions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for flight crew to maintain personal records of their duties.
subpolicies.control: Flight crew members must maintain personal records through a computerized system with audit trails.
